entries.title: Sleep paralysis · REM awakening
entries.summary: REM muscle atonia can persist briefly after cortical wake-up, leaving awareness with immobile limbs.

entries.title: Hypnic jerk · N1 onset
entries.summary: A transient motor burst during wake-to-N1 transition, often paired with a falling percept and brief arousal.

entries.title: Hypnagogic tachycardia · N1
entries.summary: Sympathetic tone can surge while cortical activity drifts into theta-range hypnagogia.

entries.title: REM atonia failure · mid-cycle
entries.summary: Incomplete inhibitory drive to spinal motor neurons during REM allows fragmentary movement.

entries.title: Sleep bruxism · N2
entries.summary: Rhythmic masticatory muscle activity often clusters around N2 with sigma-range spindle context.

entries.title: Night sweats · N3
entries.summary: Thermoregulatory and sympathetic shifts during deep NREM can produce heavy sweating without full awakening.

entries.title: Hypnopompic surge · REM exit
entries.summary: As REM ends, rising aminergic tone can collide with residual cholinergic REM state, producing a body-wide surge.

entries.title: Phasic REM eye bursts
entries.summary: Phasic REM packs ponto-geniculo-occipital-related bursts with rapid eye movements under preserved somatic atonia.

entries.title: K-complex micro-arousal · N2
entries.summary: High-amplitude K-complexes can mark protective or reactive cortical events with tiny somatic accompaniments.

entries.title: Sleep spindle density · N2
entries.summary: Thalamocortical sigma spindles gate sensory flow; somatic quietude often tracks dense spindle trains.

entries.title: Slow-wave stillness · N3
entries.summary: Delta-dominant N3 couples high homeostatic sleep pressure with low motor output.

entries.title: REM HRV constriction
entries.summary: Phasic REM can tighten beat-to-beat variability while skeletal atonia stays intact.

entries.title: Limb-float hypnagogia · N1
entries.summary: Proprioceptive drift in N1 can feel like floating as muscle tone and sensory gating shift.

entries.title: PLMS-like kicks · N2
entries.summary: Stereotyped leg movements can punctuate N2 with brief cortical arousals after each burst.

entries.title: Onset breathing pause · N1
entries.summary: Respiratory drive can briefly wobble at sleep onset as chemoreflex and upper-airway tone reconfigure.

entries.title: REM vascular engorgement
entries.summary: Autonomic REM physiology includes genital engorgement cycles independent of waking sexual context.

entries.title: Early-night N3 stillness
entries.summary: First-cycle slow-wave sleep often shows maximal stillness aligned with endocrine recovery windows.

entries.title: Fragmented REM micro-arousals
entries.summary: Interrupted REM shows mixed EEG and incomplete atonia as wake-promoting systems intrude.

entries.title: Alpha intrusion · N2
entries.summary: Alpha-range intrusion into NREM can leave the body still while subjective sleep feels shallow.

entries.title: Tonic REM stillness
entries.summary: Between phasic bursts, tonic REM holds skeletal atonia with theta-leaning cortical activity.

entries.title: Microsleep nod · N1
entries.summary: Seconds-long N1 intrusions produce postural collapse markers under high sleep pressure.

entries.title: N3 exit motor confusion
entries.summary: Forced exit from delta sleep can leave motor systems online while cortical clarity lags.

entries.title: REM temperature deregulation
entries.summary: Thermoregulatory responses are blunted in REM, so ambient shifts feel more abrupt somatically.

entries.title: Swallow burst · N2
entries.summary: Protective airway reflexes can fire in N2 with short-lived neck and laryngeal markers.

entries.title: Pre-REM atonia ramp · N2
entries.summary: Approaching REM, inhibitory drive to motor neurons ramps while EEG still shows N2 features.

entries.title: Awakening motor readiness · N1
entries.summary: Late-sleep lightening into N1/wake aligns endocrine rise with returning motor readiness.

entries.title: Sawtooth-wave REM onset
entries.summary: Sawtooth waves often herald REM entry as atonia consolidates and eye movements begin.

entries.title: N3 relative bradycardia
entries.summary: Deep NREM favors parasympathetic dominance and slower heart rate with minimal movement.

entries.title: Exploding-head burst · N1
entries.summary: A brief auditory/sensory bang at the wake–sleep border without external sound — a transition phenomenon.

entries.title: Distal REM twitches
entries.summary: Phasic REM allows brief distal motor breakthroughs while proximal atonia largely holds.
